records.ety: [Originally the same word as scuffle, and properly a freq. of shove. See Shove, and Scuffle.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shuffled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shuffling (?).]

defs: To shove one way and the other; to push from one to another; as, to shuffle money from hand to hand.
To mix by pushing or shoving; to confuse; to throw into disorder; especially, to change the relative positions of, as of the cards in a pack.
quotes: A man may shuffle cards or rattle dice from noon to midnight without tracing a new idea in his mind.

defs: To remove or introduce by artificial confusion.
quotes: It was contrived by your enemies, and shuffled into the papers that were seizen.

defs: To change the relative position of cards in a pack; as, to shuffle and cut.
To change one's position; to shift ground; to evade questions; to resort to equivocation; to prevaricate.
quotes: I myself, . . . hiding mine honor in my necessity, am fain to shuffle.

defs: To use arts or expedients; to make shift.
quotes: Your life, good master, Must shuffle for itself.

defs: To move in a slovenly, dragging manner; to drag or scrape the feet in walking or dancing.
quotes: The aged creature came Shuffling along with ivory-headed wand.

defs: The act of shuffling; a mixing confusedly; a slovenly, dragging motion.
quotes: The unguided agitation and rude shuffles of matter.

defs: A trick; an artifice; an evasion.
quotes: The gifts of nature are beyond all shame and shuffles.
